title: Communauté d'agglomération Sarreguemines Confluences
summary: Communauté d'agglomération Sarreguemines Confluences is the communauté d'agglomération, an intercommunal structure, centred on the town of Sarreguemines. It is located in the Moselle and Bas-Rhin departments, in the Grand Est region, northeastern France. Founded in 1999 and modified by the RCT law in 2010, its seat is in Sarreguemines. Its area is 340.5 km2. Its population was 64,271 in 2019, of which 20,635 in Sarreguemines proper.
text:
Communauté d'agglomération Sarreguemines Confluences is the communauté d'agglomération, an intercommunal structure, centred on the town of Sarreguemines. It is located in the Moselle and Bas-Rhin departments, in the Grand Est region, northeastern France. Founded in 1999 and modified by the RCT law in 2010, its seat is in Sarreguemines. Its area is 340.5 km2. Its population was 64,271 in 2019, of which 20,635 in Sarreguemines proper.

Composition
The communauté d'agglomération consists of the following 38 communes, of which 1 (Siltzheim) in Bas-Rhin:
